title: mappia racemosa subsp. brachycarpa
intro: Mappia racemosa subsp. brachycarpa is a plant species, also known as the Himalayan Mappia, which belongs to the family Rutaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties. The plant is valued for its health benefits, such as relieving digestive issues, reducing fever, and providing relief from respiratory problems like bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of Mappia racemosa subsp. brachycarpa include infusion, decoction, and tincture, which can be used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
